reasoning_chain_text: 1. For Chicago public elementary schools, compute the qualifying school set for each required progress-report condition in 2011-2012, 2012-2013, 2013-2014, and 2017-2018, then keep schools that satisfy all four conditions.
2. Determine the Chicago community area for the schools that remain after the progress-report filters.
3. From those community areas, keep the one with per capita income above $50,000.
4. For that community area, identify the MLB stadium located there and the team that plays at it.
5. Return the year that MLB team was founded.
original_final_question: In Chicago elementary schools that are public school, find which school (1) received the top performance rating in 2011-12, (2) showed above-average growth in the 2012–13, and (3) classified as moderately organized under the My Voice, My School measure, and (4) showed above-average or far-above-average student growth in 2017–2018, based on progress report cards. This school is also located in a community area with a per capita income above $50K. In what year was the MLB team founded that plays in the stadium located within that same community area? Please return the year only. Your response should be in the format [Answer].
original_reasoning_chain: HOP 1 (d=4, data.gov CPS progress reports - nodes 1-4):
Node 1: 2011-2012 CPS Progress Report Cards, elementary schools with CPS Performance Policy Level "Level 1" -> 127 schools
Node 2: 2012-2013 CPS elementary progress report card, Growth Overall Level "Above Average" -> 230 schools
Node 3: 2013-2014 CPS elementary progress report, My Voice, My School Overall Rating "MODERATELY ORGANIZED" -> 42 schools
Node 4: 2017-2018 CPS progress reports, Student_Growth_Rating "ABOVE AVERAGE" or "FAR ABOVE AVERAGE" -> 299 schools
Intersection of nodes 1-4: {Cleveland, Hawthorne, Poe, Reinberg} (4 schools)
HOP 2 (d=2, data.gov School Locations + Census - nodes 5-6):
Node 5 (School Locations SY2015-16): Community areas for the 4 schools -> Irving Park, Lake View, Pullman, Portage Park
Node 6 (Census Data): Per capita income > $50,000 -> Lake View only
Result: Hawthorne is the only school in a community area with per capita income > $50,000 (Lake View)
Node 7: Wrigley Field is in the Lakeview community area and is the home ballpark of the Chicago Cubs
Node 8: The Chicago Cubs were established in 1876

ideal_code.intent: Which elementary schools had CPS Performance Policy Level "Level 1" in the 2011-2012 CPS Progress Report Cards?
ideal_code.code:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = 'datagov/chicago-public-schools-progress-report-cards-2011-2012/files/rows.txt'
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)
mask = df['CPS Performance Policy Level'].isin(['Level 1'])
mask = mask & (df['Elementary, Middle, or High School'] == 'ES')
filtered = df[mask].copy()
assert len(filtered) == 127
lookup = dict(zip(filtered['School Name'].astype(str), filtered['School ID'].astype(str)))
answer = {
    "Cleveland": lookup["Cleveland"],
    "Hawthorne": lookup["Hawthorne"],
    "Poe": lookup["Poe"],
    "Reinberg": lookup["Reinberg"],
    'and 123 others': "...",
}

ideal_code.intent: Which Chicago Public Schools had Student_Growth_Rating "ABOVE AVERAGE" or "FAR ABOVE AVERAGE" in school year 2017-18?
ideal_code.code:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = 'datagov/chicago-public-schools-school-progress-reports-sy1718/files/rows.txt'
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)
mask = df['Student_Growth_Rating'].isin(['ABOVE AVERAGE', 'FAR ABOVE AVERAGE'])
filtered = df[mask].copy()
assert len(filtered) == 299
lookup = dict(zip(filtered['Short_Name'].astype(str), filtered['School_ID'].astype(str)))
answer = {
    "Cleveland": lookup["Cleveland"],
    "Hawthorne": lookup["Hawthorne"],
    "Poe": lookup["Poe"],
    "Reinberg": lookup["Reinberg"],
    'and 295 others': "...",
}

ideal_code.intent: In which community areas are the schools that meet all four progress-report criteria located, according to CPS School Locations SY2015-16?
ideal_code.code:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/chicago-public-schools-school-locations-sy1516/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)
school_ids = [609857, 609974, 610132, 610145]
mask = df["School_ID"].isin(school_ids)
name_map = {"CLEVELAND": "Cleveland", "HAWTHORNE": "Hawthorne", "POE": "Poe", "REINBERG": "Reinberg"}
area_map = {
    row["Short_Name"]: str(row["Community_Area_Name"]).title()
    for _, row in df.loc[mask].iterrows()
}
answer = {name_map[name]: area_map[name] for name in ["CLEVELAND", "HAWTHORNE", "POE", "REINBERG"]}

ideal_code.intent: Which of those community areas has per capita income above $50,000?
ideal_code.code:
import io
import pandas as pd
import boto3
from botocore import UNSIGNED
from botocore.config import Config

source = "datagov/census-data-selected-socioeconomic-indicators-in-chicago-2008-2012/files/rows.txt"
bucket = "lakeqa-yc4103-datalake"
obj = boto3.client("s3", config=Config(signature_version=UNSIGNED)).get_object(Bucket=bucket, Key=source)
df = pd.read_csv(io.BytesIO(obj["Body"].read()), low_memory=False)

community_areas = ["Irving Park", "Lake View", "Pullman", "Portage Park"]
mask = (
    df["COMMUNITY AREA NAME"].isin(community_areas)
    & (df["PER CAPITA INCOME "] > 50000)
)

answer = dict(
    zip(
        df.loc[mask, "COMMUNITY AREA NAME"],
        df.loc[mask, "PER CAPITA INCOME "].map(lambda x: f"${x:,.0f}")
    )
)
print(answer)

ideal_query.intent: Which elementary schools had CPS Performance Policy Level "Level 1" in the 2011-2012 CPS Progress Report Cards?
ideal_query.sql:
WITH filtered AS (
  SELECT CAST(TRY_CAST("School ID" AS BIGINT) AS VARCHAR) AS id
  FROM t
  WHERE "CPS Performance Policy Level" = 'Level 1' AND "Elementary, Middle, or High School" = 'ES'
), vals AS (
  SELECT 'Cleveland' AS key, max(CASE WHEN id = '609857' THEN id END) AS value FROM filtered
  UNION ALL SELECT 'Hawthorne', max(CASE WHEN id = '609974' THEN id END) FROM filtered
  UNION ALL SELECT 'Poe', max(CASE WHEN id = '610132' THEN id END) FROM filtered
  UNION ALL SELECT 'Reinberg', max(CASE WHEN id = '610145' THEN id END) FROM filtered
  UNION ALL SELECT 'and 123 others', '...'
)
SELECT to_json(map(list(key), list(value))) AS answer FROM vals

ideal_query.intent: Which schools in the 2012-2013 CPS elementary progress report card have Growth Overall Level "Above Average"?
ideal_query.sql:
WITH filtered AS (
  SELECT upper(trim(CAST("School Short Name" AS VARCHAR))) AS name_key, CAST(TRY_CAST("School ID" AS BIGINT) AS VARCHAR) AS id
  FROM t
  WHERE "Growth Overall Level" = 'Above Average'
), vals AS (
  SELECT 'Cleveland' AS key, max(CASE WHEN name_key = 'CLEVELAND' THEN id END) AS value FROM filtered
  UNION ALL SELECT 'Hawthorne', max(CASE WHEN name_key = 'HAWTHORNE' THEN id END) FROM filtered
  UNION ALL SELECT 'Poe', max(CASE WHEN name_key = 'POE' THEN id END) FROM filtered
  UNION ALL SELECT 'Reinberg', max(CASE WHEN name_key = 'REINBERG' THEN id END) FROM filtered
  UNION ALL SELECT 'and 226 others', '...'
)
SELECT to_json(map(list(key), list(value))) AS answer FROM vals

ideal_query.intent: Which Chicago Public Schools had Student_Growth_Rating "ABOVE AVERAGE" or "FAR ABOVE AVERAGE" in school year 2017-18?
ideal_query.sql:
WITH filtered AS (
  SELECT upper(trim(CAST("Short_Name" AS VARCHAR))) AS name_key, CAST(TRY_CAST("School_ID" AS BIGINT) AS VARCHAR) AS id
  FROM t
  WHERE Student_Growth_Rating IN ('ABOVE AVERAGE', 'FAR ABOVE AVERAGE')
), vals AS (
  SELECT 'Cleveland' AS key, max(CASE WHEN name_key = 'CLEVELAND' THEN id END) AS value FROM filtered
  UNION ALL SELECT 'Hawthorne', max(CASE WHEN name_key = 'HAWTHORNE' THEN id END) FROM filtered
  UNION ALL SELECT 'Poe', max(CASE WHEN name_key = 'POE' THEN id END) FROM filtered
  UNION ALL SELECT 'Reinberg', max(CASE WHEN name_key = 'REINBERG' THEN id END) FROM filtered
  UNION ALL SELECT 'and 295 others', '...'
)
SELECT to_json(map(list(key), list(value))) AS answer FROM vals

ideal_query.intent: In which community areas are the schools that meet all four progress-report criteria located, according to CPS School Locations SY2015-16?
ideal_query.sql:
WITH vals AS (
  SELECT CASE trim(Short_Name)
      WHEN 'CLEVELAND' THEN 'Cleveland'
      WHEN 'HAWTHORNE' THEN 'Hawthorne'
      WHEN 'POE' THEN 'Poe'
      WHEN 'REINBERG' THEN 'Reinberg'
    END AS key,
    array_to_string(list_transform(string_split(lower(trim(COMMAREA)), ' '), x -> upper(substr(x,1,1)) || substr(x,2)), ' ') AS value
  FROM t
  WHERE TRY_CAST(School_ID AS BIGINT) IN (609857,609974,610132,610145)
)
SELECT to_json(map(list(key), list(value))) AS answer FROM vals WHERE key IS NOT NULL

ideal_query.intent: Which of those community areas has per capita income above $50,000?
ideal_query.sql:
SELECT to_json(map(list(key), list(value))) AS answer
FROM (
  SELECT 'Lake View' AS key,
         '$' || substr(CAST(TRY_CAST("PER CAPITA INCOME" AS BIGINT) AS VARCHAR), 1, 2) || ',' || substr(CAST(TRY_CAST("PER CAPITA INCOME" AS BIGINT) AS VARCHAR), 3) AS value
  FROM t
  WHERE trim("Community Area Name") = 'Lake View'
    AND TRY_CAST("PER CAPITA INCOME" AS BIGINT) > 50000
) q
